Hey, welcome back. If you're like most people I work with, this is the module you've been truly waiting for, the five elements of the straight line system. This is where I actually break down the straight line view into its most basic elements and also show you the basic tools of human influence that we use to actually take someone down the straight line as the phrase goes from the open to the close. Now who's ever had a sale that was absolutely perfect, a lay down where they were almost pre-sold before you opened up your mouth? We all had those, right? The problem is that those sales are far and few between. Typically what happens is your prospect tries to take you off the straight line and take control of the actual conversation. So what we have are these actual boundaries here and here above and below the line. When you're within these boundaries you're in control of the sale, when you're outside the boundaries, the clients can control the sale and they're talking about whatever they want to talk about which could be the price of tea in China, politics in America, their latest objection, whatever it might be, a stall and you're not influencing anymore because they're the ones who are in control. Now when you're in control which is what you absolutely must be, it does not mean not letting your clients speak. In fact it's the exact opposite. But the key is to actually control the flow of the encounter. If you're in the boundaries here and here you're in control. If you're outside the boundaries then they're in control and you've gone up into Pluto or down here which is your anus which is not a very good place to be if you want to influence. You're trying to keep them on this straight line, moving them from the open to the close. In essence what the straight line is, it's a visual representation of the perfect sale where they say yes to basically everything you say. You ask them a question and they're like, oh yeah, it sounds great. Ask them another question and they're like, oh of course, yeah, perfect, terrific, absolutely. Give them the price of your product and they're like, well that sounds pretty reasonable. Here's my credit card information and that's that. That's the perfect straight line conversation. It's almost a one sided conversation with a perfect lay down client where you're presenting and they're simply agreeing with everything you say. But that's not the way it really works in sales now is it? You see you have these spaces here and here above and below the line. These are your healthy boundaries. Believe it or not, right in here, boom, boom, that's where all the magic happens when you're actually off the straight line, right in these spaces. In fact, there are not just one but two absolutely crucial things happening here. First, number one, developing immediate massive rapport with your prospect on both the conscious and an unconscious level. And second, you're using that rapport to gather massive intelligence. And in fact, when you're gathering intelligence from a prospect, you're looking at all of the following things. Number one, they're identifying their needs and this includes not just their primary need or their problem so to speak, but also any secondary ancillary needs they might have, any core beliefs they might have that might impact the sale. You're looking to identify their values, their standards, their past experiences with similar products, their past experiences with other salespeople, where they stand financially and of course, where their pain is precisely. And then if necessary, amplifying that pain. Now I'll go into this in intimate detail in the prospecting module and I'm going to hand you this entire chunk of content literally on a silver platter. So again, when you're off the line, you're looking to A, number one, to continue to build that massive rapport you already have and B, use that rapport to help you gather massive intelligence. At the same time, you're making sure that the encounter stays within the boundaries as you slowly but surely continue the process of moving down the straight line towards the close. Essentially, those are the three tenets of the front half of the straight line. Let me repeat them again. Number one, first you must get into instant massive rapport on both the conscious and an unconscious level. Two, you then use that rapport to essentially lubricate the intelligence gathering process which includes both identifying his needs and uncovering his pain. And three, you must control the sale by not letting it go outside the boundaries to either Pluto or your anus. Now remember, like I said in the intro, you have that very strong good enough factor with the straight line. So if you're becoming even reasonably proficient at it, you're going to find yourself with a massive increase in your closing rate and also you'll be setting yourself up for a massive number of referrals. Now real quick, before I go any further, let me just give you another quick caution here about ethics. Remember, the straight lines are really powerful too. It's actually one of those rare things that works better in the real world than it does on paper. So if you become even reasonably good at it, you can easily get people to do things they shouldn't do, buy things they shouldn't buy, or buy more of what they should buy. Like, maybe your cusp needs a bag of soil but you end up using the straight line to sell them a dump truck of it, right? Get the picture. Always remember your ethics and your integrity. Never sacrifice either for the sake of making a sale.
